sentence_reviews.text: Learning-rate schedules, warmup, clipping, initialization, gradient scaling, and related controls keep optimization inside numerically stable regimes.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapter 8 optimization strategies, learning rates, initialization, and gradient clipping/exploding-gradient discussion.
sentence_reviews.notes: Learning rates, initialization, and clipping are supported as optimization/training controls. Warmup and gradient scaling are not directly supported by the 2016 source, and 'numerically stable regimes' is broad.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Learning-rate schedules, warmup & stabilization is to connect Adam, Adafactor & adaptive optimizers, Normalization & standardization to downstream capabilities including Large-scale pretraining.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapter 8 optimization and Chapter 8.7 batch normalization; Chapter 11 practical methodology.
sentence_reviews.notes: Optimizer and normalization connections are partially supported. Adafactor, warmup, and large-scale pretraining are not directly supported by the cited textbook.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapter 11 practical methodology and Chapter 8 optimization for training deep models.
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected source predates modern large-scale pretraining practice and does not directly support this downstream claim.

sentence_reviews.text: combined with Adam, Adafactor & adaptive optimizers
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapter 8.5 adaptive learning rates and broader learning-rate/optimization discussion.
sentence_reviews.notes: Adam and adaptive learning-rate context are supported; Adafactor and warmup-specific combination are not.

sentence_reviews.text: Results are sensitive to data quality, hyperparameters, compute budget, random variation, and evaluation contamination. Its direct dependencies include Adam, Adafactor & adaptive optimizers, Normalization & standardization.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapters 5, 8, and 11 on generalization, optimization, hyperparameters, and practical methodology.
sentence_reviews.notes: Hyperparameter and optimization sensitivity are supported. Data quality, compute budget, random variation, evaluation contamination, and Adafactor are not directly supported.

sentence_reviews.text: Learning-rate schedules, warmup & stabilization uses Adam, Adafactor & adaptive optimizers as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapter 8.5 adaptive learning rates and optimization strategy sections.
sentence_reviews.notes: Adaptive learning-rate methods are relevant, but schedules/stabilization do not necessarily use adaptive optimizers, and Adafactor is not sourced.

sentence_reviews.text: Learning-rate schedules, warmup & stabilization uses Normalization & standardization as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapter 8.7 batch normalization and optimization-stabilization context.
sentence_reviews.notes: Batch normalization is presented as improving optimization/training behavior. The reusable-ingredient phrasing is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: Learning-rate schedules, warmup & stabilization uses Numerical analysis as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapter 8 optimization, numerical gradient behavior, and training stability discussion.
sentence_reviews.notes: Stability controls for iterative optimization are numerical in nature; the exact dependency wording is synthesized.

edge_reviews.notes: Adaptive learning-rate methods are related, but schedules/warmup/stabilization do not require Adam-like optimizers. Core strength is not well supported.
Normalization is a common stabilization technique for training; core strength is plausible but not exclusive.
Iterative training stability is a numerical optimization issue; common strength is defensible.
